originalTitle: Nearly 50 people die of thirst in Sahara desert after lorry breaks down
title: A Sad Story in the Big Desert: Brave Friends and the Power of Water
content:
Hi there, wonderful kids of Africa! It’s your friend, Reporter Roho, bringing you news from our amazing continent. Today, I have a story from a very big and hot place called the Sahara Desert. It’s a sad story, but it also shows how brave people can be, and how important water is for all of us.

The Big, Big Desert

Imagine a place with sand everywhere. So much sand! And the sun shines super, super hot all day long. This is the Sahara Desert. It's one of the biggest deserts in the whole world! It covers many parts of Africa. It can be a beautiful place, with big sand dunes like mountains, but it can also be a very tough place to live or travel through. Did you know? The Sahara Desert is so big, it's almost as large as the whole United States of America! That's HUGE!

A Journey in a Big Lorry

A long time ago, some people were traveling through this big desert. They were riding in a very big truck, which we sometimes call a 'lorry' in Africa. These people were going on a journey, maybe to find a new home, or to visit family, or to find work. Journeys can be very exciting, but also very long, especially in the desert. Simple Explanation: A lorry is just a very big truck that can carry many people or things over long distances.

Trouble on the Road

But then, something went wrong. The big lorry broke down. When a car or a lorry breaks down, it means it cannot move anymore. It stops working. This was a very, very big problem because they were in the middle of the desert. There were no towns nearby, no shops, no other cars passing by. Just sand and the hot sun. Did you know? Sometimes, lorries break down because of old parts, or a flat tyre, or just because the desert roads are very bumpy and tough!

No Water, Big Problem!

When the lorry stopped, the people were stuck. And the biggest problem was water. They did not have enough water to drink. Our bodies need water to live, just like a car needs fuel to move. If we don't drink water, especially when it's hot, we can get very, very sick. We get super thirsty, our mouths feel dry, and our bodies get tired. Simple Explanation: Thirst is your body telling you, "Please, give me water!" When you get too thirsty for too long, it's called 'dehydration', and it's very dangerous.

Brave Friends Walk for Help

There were many people stuck with the broken lorry. But two brave people decided they had to do something. They knew they had to find help. So, they started to walk. And walk. And walk! Imagine walking for a very, very long time. They walked over 50 kilometres. That's like walking from one big town to another! It’s a very, very long way. Did you know? 50 kilometres is like walking for more than ten hours without stopping, and even longer in the hot sun and sand! It takes a lot of courage and strength.

A Long, Hard Walk

The sun was hot on their heads. The sand was hot under their feet. They were very tired and very, very thirsty. But they kept going. Why? Because they knew that if they didn't find help, everyone else would be in big trouble. They were thinking of their friends and family back at the broken lorry. They were so, so brave.

Finding Help at Last

After a very, very long and hard walk, these two brave friends finally found someone! They found people who could help. They told them about the broken lorry and all the people stuck in the desert without water. Immediately, the people who heard the story sent out help. They sent cars to find the others. Simple Explanation: 'Authorities' are like the police or the rescue teams who help people when they are in trouble.

A Sad Outcome

When the help arrived at the broken lorry, it was a very sad moment. Most of the people who were with the lorry had become very, very sick from not having water for so long. Nearly 50 people had died because of thirst. This is a very sad part of the story, and it shows us just how powerful and dangerous the desert can be if we are not careful.

Lessons from the Desert

This sad story teaches us some very important things. First, water is so, so precious. We must always make sure we have enough water, especially when we travel, or when it's very hot. Always carry extra water! Second, the desert is a beautiful place, but we must respect it. It can be very harsh. Third, these two brave friends showed us what it means to be courageous and to help others. They risked their own lives to try and save others. Did you know? People can live for many days without food, but only a few days without water! Water is THAT important.

How We Can Be Safe

When people travel in big, empty places like the desert, it's important to be prepared. * They should have lots of water. * They should have food. * They should have a way to call for help, like a special phone. * They should travel with people who know the desert well. * And their cars or lorries should be strong and checked before a long trip. Simple Explanation: 'Prepared' means you have everything you need ready before you start something, so you are safe.

Thinking of Others

This story makes us think about people who have to travel far, sometimes through difficult places. We should always be kind and helpful to others. We should also remember how important water is and never waste it. Every drop counts!

A Message from Reporter Roho

So, remember kids, water is life! And bravery can make a big difference. Let's all be kind, be safe, and always, always drink enough water. Until next time, stay curious and stay kind!
summary: In the huge Sahara Desert, a lorry broke down, leaving many people without water; two very brave friends walked a very long way to get help, but sadly, most people died of thirst, showing us how important water is.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us how important water is for our bodies to live, how brave people can be to help others, and why we must always be careful and prepared when traveling, especially in big, hot places like the desert.

originalTitle: Fall in official Ebola numbers appears to be good news but it's not that simple
title: Fall in official Ebola numbers appears to be good news but it's not that simple
content: There are now 380 confirmed cases of Ebola in DR Congo, far lower than initial estimates of suspected cases, writes Fergus Walsh.

content: He follows the late great Papa Wemba in becoming a knight of the National Order of the Leopard.

title: Iran footballers issued US visas for World Cup, says White House
content: Iran players get visas 10 days before their opening ‌World Cup match against New Zealand in Los Angeles on June 15.

title: Zelenskyy: Putin choosing war by rejecting offer for in-person talks
content: Russian President Vladimir Putin has turned down an offer for in-person talks with the Ukrainian President.

originalTitle: Trump hails jobs surge, says Iran talks ‘going well’
title: Trump hails jobs surge, says Iran talks ‘going well’
content: Trump hailed jobs growth before pivoting to Iran, saying negotiations with Tehran "seem to be going quite well".

originalTitle: Putin says there is 'no point' meeting Zelensky over ending Ukraine war
title: Putin says there is 'no point' meeting Zelensky over ending Ukraine war
content: The Russian president's refusal comes after his Ukrainian counterpart wrote an open letter calling for face-to-face talks.

originalTitle: Ukraine strikes cargo ships and admits Romania drone blast
title: Ukraine strikes cargo ships and admits Romania drone blast
content: Ukraine says it struck five ships carrying illegal cargo in the Sea of Azov and in coastal waters of Russian-occupied territories.

originalTitle: Astronauts return to ISS after sheltering during air leak repair attempt
title: Astronauts return to ISS after sheltering during air leak repair attempt
content: Russian attempt to repair tunnel area sparks safe-haven procedure for five other astronauts onboard.

originalTitle: O'Neill set to stay on as Celtic manager
title: O'Neill set to stay on as Celtic manager
content: Martin O'Neill will continue as Celtic manager after leading the side to a Premiership and Scottish Cup double.

originalTitle: Mowbray returns as Blackburn head coach
title: Mowbray returns as Blackburn head coach
content: Blackburn Rovers appoint Tony Mowbray as their new head coach for a second spell in charge.

originalTitle: Tuchel will not let Tampa pitch affect selection
title: Tuchel will not let Tampa pitch affect selection
content: England head coach Thomas Tuchel says reports about the state of the pitch at the venue for his side's friendly against New Zealand will not influence team selection.
